title: Project Overview
description: OriStudio is a locally-run B/S desktop application for independent creators that covers the full creative-to-monetization lifecycle: asset management, copyright registration, infringement monitoring, IP registration, supply chain collaboration, and publishing. This README introduces the architecture, tech stack, quick-start instructions, and phased development roadmap, giving you the lay of the land for the rest of the tour.
languageLesson: Documentation-driven onboarding helps newcomers grasp project scope before diving into code. A well-structured README covers: what the project does, who it serves, the technology stack, how to run it locally, and the directory structure -- this lets you navigate to the right layer without guessing.

title: Backend Entry Point
description: The FastAPI application factory bootstraps the entire server: it configures CORS, GZip compression, the middleware stack (audit logging, request timing), mounts static file serving, and registers all API routers under the /api prefix. This single file is where you see every subsystem plug in for the first time.
languageLesson: FastAPI apps use APIRouter objects to modularize routes -- each router module defines its own endpoint functions with type-annotated request/response models. The main.py aggregates them via app.include_router(), keeping the entry point clean while enabling parallel development across teams.

title: Configuration & Database
description: Application settings flow from environment variables through a Pydantic-based config model, covering database URLs, API keys, CORS origins, and JWT configuration. The database layer sits on SQLAlchemy 2.0 with SQLite in WAL mode, featuring optimized PRAGMAs for concurrent reads and foreign key enforcement -- ideal for a locally-run desktop application.

title: Core Data Model
description: The Work model is the heart of the application -- every creative work has a title, description, category, media file, thumbnail, tags, project linkage, and a status progression pipeline. All other domain models (copyright, monitoring, IP registration, supply chain) relate back to Work, making this the anchor for understanding the entire domain vocabulary.

title: Authentication Layer
description: The deps.py module provides shared FastAPI dependencies for authentication: token verification via HMAC-signed JWTs, user lookup from the database, and bearer token extraction. Every protected route injects these dependencies, centralizing security logic so individual routers stay focused on business logic.

title: API Routers and Services
description: The works router exemplifies the API layer with 33 endpoints covering full CRUD, AI auto-tagging, rights declarations, tag management, batch operations, preview generation, and bulk folder import. The auto-tagging service underneath uses filename keywords, EXIF data, and file-type mapping to suggest industry-standard tags -- a concrete example of the service layer bridging business logic with external data sources.

title: Gateway Adapters
description: The gateway module implements the adapter pattern for all third-party integrations: copy detection engines (Copyscape, Baidu Vision, Google Vision), blockchain notary services (ZhixinChain, AntChain), and print-on-demand platforms (Printful, Redbubble, Zazzle). Each gateway extends a base class defining a uniform contract, making it easy to add new providers without touching existing router code.
languageLesson: The gateway adapter pattern wraps heterogeneous external APIs behind a uniform interface. A base class or protocol defines the method signatures (e.g., search(content), register(work)), and each concrete implementation handles its provider's authentication, rate limiting, and response parsing -- decoupling the business logic from vendor-specific quirks.

title: Background Task Queue
description: Celery workers handle long-running operations asynchronously: file hashing with WebSocket progress reporting, scheduled monitoring scans, batch certificate generation, and automated data backups. The task module defines a Beat schedule for periodic jobs, while the WebSocket router pushes real-time progress updates to connected frontend clients.

title: API Contract Enforcement
description: Pydantic schemas define the request/response contract between frontend and backend. The common schemas module provides shared response wrappers (ApiResponse, PaginatedResponse) used by every endpoint. Strong typing here catches malformed requests early and gives the frontend a reliable interface to build against.

title: Frontend Bootstrap
description: The Vue 3 frontend bootstraps via main.ts, wiring up Pinia for state management and Vue Router for navigation. The router defines 17 route mappings covering the landing page, authentication, dashboard, and all six major feature modules. An auth guard middleware intercepts unauthenticated navigation, mirroring the backend's protection strategy on the client side.

title: HTTP Client Layer
description: The Axios client wraps every backend API call with a unified interface: JWT token injection into request headers, automatic error-to-toast notification forwarding, and response envelope unwrapping. Each feature module (works, auth, monitor, IPR, supply, publish) gets its own typed API client module, keeping HTTP concerns out of views and stores.

title: Client State Management
description: Pinia stores manage the client-side data flow for each domain. The work store exemplifies the pattern: it handles listing, fetching, uploading, updating, deleting, restoring, and trashing creative works, with built-in filtering and pagination. The work types file mirrors the backend's Work model in TypeScript, ensuring type safety across the full stack.

title: Composable Utilities
description: Vue 3 composables encapsulate reusable client-side logic: file upload with drag-and-drop validation and progress tracking, internationalization with zh-CN/en-US dictionaries, modal state management with keyboard support, toast notifications, and debounced tag input with autocomplete. Each composable follows the composition API pattern, keeping UI logic composable and testable.

title: Containerization & Deployment
description: The Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build to produce a lean Python 3.11 production image with FFmpeg and uvicorn exposing port 8765. docker-compose orchestrates the full stack: the backend API service, Redis cache for the Celery broker, a Celery worker, and a Celery beat scheduler -- all sharing environment variables and Redis volume persistence for local development and staging.
languageLesson: docker-compose groups related services into a single development environment. Each service declares its own build context, port mappings, environment variables, and volume mounts. The Redis broker connects the Celery worker and beat to the API, enabling async task processing without changing any application code -- just swapping from in-process to distributed execution.

title: Architecture Summary
description: You now have a complete mental model of OriStudio's layered architecture. The Entry layer bootstraps both the FastAPI server and Vue SPA. The Data and Types layers define the persistent domain model on both sides. The Service and API layers implement business logic and expose REST endpoints. The UI, State, and Utility layers handle the frontend presentation. Finally, the Infrastructure layer ties everything together with Docker and configuration. This separation of concerns lets each layer evolve independently -- new gateway adapters, new frontend views, and new background tasks all plug in without touching the others.
